article_items.description: We’re announcing a multi-year global alliance with DXC Technology, one of the world’s largest IT services companies.

DXC will train tens of thousands of Claude-certified forward-deployed engineers (FDEs) - engineers embedded directly inside customer organizations - to bring Claude into the systems DXC operates for the world’s largest banks, airlines, insurers, manufacturers, and government agencies. “For more than 50 years, DXC and the companies it was built from run the systems that run the world,” said Raul Fernandez, President and CEO, DXC Technology.

article_items.title: PhysicsIntern: from an Autonomous Benchmark-runner to a Research Sidekick
article_items.description: A Blog post by David Louapre on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by David Louapre on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: That single move of recognising the question is ill-posed and fixing it is precisely the kind of judgment a benchmark can never reward, and precisely what you want from a research partner. A Blog post by David Louapre on Hugging Face PhysicsIntern: from an Autonomous Benchmark-runner to a Research Sidekick A few weeks ago we released physics-intern , an autonomous agent for physics research . The plan is the moment where a researcher's judgment is worth the most and the agent's is worth the least, so that's where the intern hands the wheel back to you. Twenty-one commits, one per step, each bundling the new artefact with the updated research log. The plan is the moment where a researcher's judgment is worth the most and the agent's is worth the least, so that's where the intern hands the wheel back to you.

A Blog post by David Louapre on Hugging Face PhysicsIntern: from an Autonomous Benchmark-runner to a Research Sidekick A few weeks ago we released physics-intern , an autonomous agent for physics research .
article_items.why_matters: The plan is the moment where a researcher's judgment is worth the most and the agent's is worth the least, so that's where the intern hands the wheel back to you. Twenty-one commits, one per step, each bundling the new artefact with the updated research log.

key_points: That single move of recognising the question is ill-posed and fixing it is precisely the kind of judgment a benchmark can never reward, and precisely what you want from a research partner.

The structured, multi-agent approach measurably outperformed the single-shot baselines, for instance bringing Kimi K2.6 from 8.0% to 21.4% on the CritPt benchmark, and lifting Gemini 3.1 Pro from 17.7% to 31.4%, higher.
The plan is the moment where a researcher's judgment is worth the most and the agent's is worth the least, so that's where the intern hands the wheel back to you.

A Blog post by David Louapre on Hugging Face PhysicsIntern: from an Autonomous Benchmark-runner to a Research Sidekick A few weeks ago we released physics-intern , an autonomous agent for physics research .
Twenty-one commits, one per step, each bundling the new artefact with the updated research log.
The session is disposable, because the files are the memory.
Then I launched Codex in the workspace and asked it to read the problem, the notes I provided, and then run a survey (which triggered the corresponding /survey skill).
Everything that matters lives in plain files in a git repository: the research log, the plan, the derivations and their reviews, the computations, the critiques, the final answer.
After each sub-agent returns, the main agent runs a tight integration loop: read the result, update research_log.md and the plan, and commit.
This ensures that no lone verdict moves the work forward, and the whole research program won’t be derailed by the mistake of a single agent.
The way you get that evidence is to run on a benchmark like CritPt , and obviously such a benchmark cannot have a human in the loop.
The run above was on Codex with GPT-5.

article_items.title: Deezer's new tool can identify AI music from Spotify, Apple Music, and others | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.
article_items.excerpt: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.
article_items.summary: The launch of the new tool comes on the heels of Deezer revealing that a staggering 44% of all new music uploaded to its platform is AI-generated. The launch further positions Deezer as one of the music industry’s most aggressive opponents of AI music, which could be a selling point for its service among consumers. To use the new tool, go to Deezer’s AI music detector website, select your streaming service, and allow Deezer to access your playlists. Around 85% of these streams are flagged as fraudulent and are demonetized by the platform. Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence.

Announced on Thursday, this free online AI music detector supports 27 languages and gives users from 20 of the most popular platforms the chance to see if their playlists include any AI-generated songs.
Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.
However, many music streaming services have yet to launch AI music detection tools.
To use the new tool, go to Deezer’s AI music detector website, select your streaming service, and allow Deezer to access your playlists.
The tool is compatible with Spotify, Apple Music, SoundCloud, and YouTube Music, among other platforms.
“By detecting and tagging AI-generated music over the past year and a half, Deezer has been at the forefront of transparency in music streaming.
In the ongoing effort to tackle this issue, Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from various streaming platforms to identify AI-generated tracks.
Around 85% of these streams are flagged as fraudulent and are demonetized by the platform.
Despite this influx, the listening rate for AI-generated music remains relatively low, accounting for just 1-3% of total streams.

Behind the scenes, nine separate data sources have been unified into a single governed lakehouse, serving hundreds of North American locations at launch in mid-April 2026.
And because the conversational agent supports approximately twelve languages at ~98% accuracy, frontline staff can interact in whichever language they're most comfortable with.

by Babu Chinnaswamy , Nicholas Dylla , Alissa Ellingson and Harish Gaur Ecolab uses Anthropic's Claude Sonnet and Haiku on Databricks Foundation Model APIs to convert 700-page FDA food safety manuals into cited.
The Unity AI Gateway layers on payload logging, per-user rate limiting, PII guardrails, and automatic fallbacks, while Unity Catalog governs access to both the data and the models serving it.
For Ecolab's customers, the outcome is measurably better compliance postures, fewer penalty events, and a partner that delivers intelligence proactively rather than reactively.

Next, Ecolab plans to add MCP-powered automated actions - pest inspections, chemical reorders, food safety norms and work orders triggered directly from the chat interface - turning the system from an intelligence layer.
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site.
But unifying the data was only half the challenge.
Profiles are stored as structured records and continuously updated as the user interacts.
Through Foundation Model APIs , Ecolab serves Claude Sonnet for complex reasoning, Haiku for fast and cost-effective summarization, and Gemini for image analysis, all from the same control plane.
All nine data sources flow through Lakeflow and Spark Declarative Pipelines into a governed lakehouse under Unity Catalog , deployed reproducibly via Databricks Asset Bundles.

article_items.title: Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B for Physical AI
article_items.description: Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B for Physical AI
article_items.excerpt: Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B for Physical AI
article_items.summary: Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B for Physical AI Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B for Physical AI Funding from investors including Nvidia, Amazon and Qualcomm will support the vendor’s development of humanoid robots and physical AI. AI hardware-software giant Nvidia, in particular, has been stepping up its push into physical AI, expanding partnerships with robotics companies including Agility Robotics, Figure AI , ABB Robotics and Kuka. The company also plans to develop its " Neura Gyms " -- a network of training environments designed to collect real-world robotics data and support the development of physical AI models. German startup Neura Robotics completed a Series C funding round of up to $1.4 billion, which the vendor claims the largest investment into a full-stack robotics company to date. “It will move, interact, learn and work beside us in the real world.” Related: Amazon Unveils Next-Generation Robot in $11.6B European Push Established in 2019, Neura’s cognitive and humanoid robots are designed to work alongside humans across industrial, commercial and domestic environments.

The funding round also included backing from investors and strategic partners Tether, Bosch, Schaeffler and the European Investment Bank.
The funding highlights growing investor interest in physical AI and robotics, as technology advancements and consumer demands call for increasingly autonomous and capable machines.

article_items.title: Lionsgate Takes Equity Stake in Runway, Will Mine IP for AI-Generated Shorts
article_items.description: Lionsgate will mine existing franchises to create AI-generated short films as part of an expanded partnership with AI company Runway.
article_items.excerpt: Lionsgate will mine existing franchises to create AI-generated short films as part of an expanded partnership with AI company Runway.

Lionsgate will mine existing franchises to create AI-generated short films as part of an expanded partnership with AI company Runway.
A timetable for when the series would be released was not given.
This expanded partnership will help more stories be told, faster.
“Runway is a great creative partner, an exciting part of our AI strategy and a valuable driver in expanding our storytelling capabilities,” said Lionsgate Vice Chairman Michael Burns in a statement.

As we continue to expand the use cases of Runway technology across our production operations and introduce its tools to more of our filmmakers, we believe it will help our talent redefine and reshape the art of the.
As part of the expansion, the companies will create AI-generated short episodic series using existing Lionsgate IP.
Now, with the announcement of these IP-driven short series, we’ll see what Lionsgate and Runway have come up with.

They did not reveal which franchises will be used for these AI series, and while some of the most popular IP in the Lionsgate library includes “John Wick” and “The Hunger Games,” there are thousands of titles that could.
